description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The block is solid and has a substantial thickness, with clean, sharp edges and a uniform, geometric shape. The stone appears to have a slightly mottled, gray coloration, suggesting a granite or concrete material. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. This type of block is commonly used as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, plazas, or as part of a modern landscape design.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The block is solid, gray, and has smooth sides with a textured, grooved top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth of the grooves and the block's substantial thickness.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped pattern from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the groove depth and the block's solid form.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a modern barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or placed directly on a leveled surface
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
